beats.activity: Wake up, strong espresso, check weather and mood before committing to the day
beats.reasoning: Emma's routine: wake early, assess conditions. Yesterday was high-energy social day; today's rain and low mood (45/100) mean she'll need caffeine and flexibility. Starting at home in Shoreline per continuity.

beats.activity: Drive to South Lake Union job site, avoid SR-520 closure by taking I-5
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound closed 9am–noon; Emma leaves early (7:45) to avoid the crunch and hit the site before rain peaks. I-5 route adds ~10 min but safer than getting stuck.

beats.activity: Install solar panels on residential roof, sheltering under eaves during heaviest rain
beats.reasoning: Steady rain 7am–2pm means slower progress and safety concerns. Emma adapts: shorter shifts in weather, work through midday when rain is heaviest (no point driving around), focus on covered tasks.

beats.activity: Grab lunch at a nearby café, warm up with soup and espresso
beats.reasoning: Rain is still steady; Emma stays local rather than driving. Needs warmth and caffeine to push through the grey day. Low energy means comfort food and staying put.

beats.activity: Resume roof work; rain lightens after 2pm, finish up early
beats.reasoning: Rain forecast clears by 2pm. Emma pushes through the afternoon shift, wraps by 2:50 before conditions deteriorate further. Low energy and yesterday's social hangover suggest she'll head home rather than seek evening social plans.

beats.activity: Drive home to Shoreline, avoiding still-congested cross-lake routes
beats.reasoning: SR-520 still recovering from morning closure. Emma takes I-5 north, direct route home. Mood is low, weather is grey—she's ready to decompress.

beats.activity: Stop at local Safeway for groceries: mushrooms (fresh and dried), cheese, bread
beats.reasoning: Close to home in Shoreline. Foraging passion means she'll pick up specialty mushrooms for tonight. Keeps the errand quick and local given the grey weather.

beats.activity: Leisurely cooking: sauté mushrooms, make a simple risotto, set the mood with music
beats.reasoning: Emma is home, mood is lower, weather is still grey. Cooking is both grounding and a form of self-care. Mushroom prep aligns with her foraging passion. No social plans tonight—just recovery.

beats.activity: Eat dinner, pour a glass of wine, scroll through weather forecast and basketball league updates
beats.reasoning: Quiet evening at home. Emma's energy is 45/100, so she's recharging rather than seeking out pickup basketball or social plans. Comfort meal, low-key evening.

beats.activity: Wind down with a book or podcast, make tea, get ready for bed
beats.reasoning: Low energy from yesterday's social high and today's grey weather. Emma is settling in early, no late-night plans. Getting into sleep routine by 10pm.
Long sleep (8.5 hours) needed to recover from yesterday's energy spike and today's lower-mood grey day. Ends just after 6am to show overnight span clearly on map replay.

diary: Woke up to that grey, relentless drizzle and honestly almost talked myself out of the whole day. But the espresso helped—it always does. The SR-520 closure had everyone in a panic, so I took I-5 instead and made it to South Lake Union by 8:30. Spent most of the morning on that residential roof in the rain, huddled under the eaves when it got really heavy. There's something meditative about it, even when you're soaked. Ducked into Café Solstice around noon for soup and warmth, then pushed through the afternoon. By 2pm the rain finally eased up and I actually finished early—a gift. Drove home through lighter traffic, grabbed mushrooms and cheese at Safeway, and spent the rest of the evening doing what I love: cooking. Made a simple risotto, poured wine, let the kitchen fill with steam and music. That's when the day transformed. Ended up reading on the couch with tea, feeling genuinely settled.
thought_process: The weather had me second-guessing everything this morning—grey days mess with my motivation—but I knew pushing through would be worth it. The bridge closure was frustrating but I've learned to adapt my routes; the alternative was sitting in cross-lake gridlock. Work felt grounded despite the rain; there's honest satisfaction in installing panels even in bad weather. I skipped a rushed lunch and gave myself time at the café instead, which shifted my whole afternoon mindset. By the time rain cleared, I was already in a better headspace. Choosing to cook rather than order takeout felt intentional—I needed to create something warm with my hands. The evening slowness was exactly what I needed after a damp, demanding day.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
